title: vittadinia dissecta
intro: Vittadinia dissecta, also known as the New South Wales daisy or Cut-leaf Vittadinia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Vittadinia dissecta is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, and is also used in traditional Australian medicine.
